behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is between modules: JSON ingestion/normalization and trace-correlation must agree on trace ID extraction and bucket keys across service-specific records. A unit test of only the grouping function can miss handoff mismatches (e.g., field mapping differences). Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text only covers local log processing; no real OS/network/subprocess and no AI/ML output dependency are described.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process pipeline test that feeds mixed JSON logs from multiple services (same trace ID, different service metadata) through parser + normalizer + correlator, then assert exactly one correlated group contains all matching entries.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when per-service timestamps, skew-adjustment logic, and timeline assembly interact. Pure unit tests of sort logic can miss incorrect offset application across modules. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because the behavior is deterministic local computation with no required real infrastructure or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Feed correlated cross-service entries with known skew patterns (within and around 500ms) through timeline-builder modules and assert final timeline order matches expected causal sequence after skew compensation.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans percentile computation, baseline comparison, and anomaly-report emission. A single-function unit test of threshold math can miss wiring failures where computed spikes are not surfaced in report output. Rejected higher levels because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess and does not involve AI/ML outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process test dataset with a stable baseline window and a spike window where p99 exceeds 3x baseline, run stats + detector + report generator together, and assert the anomaly report includes a latency-spike flag with correct metric context.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is parser error handling propagated into pipeline resilience and metrics reporting. Unit tests of parser exceptions alone can miss dropped/incorrect skip-count propagation. Rejected System because malformed-input handling can be reproduced fully in-process; rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model-dependent behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline modules together on a mixed batch of valid and malformed JSON lines; assert processing completes, valid entries continue downstream, and reported skipped-entry count equals malformed-input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: Throughput/backpressure under burst load depends on real runtime scheduling, queue behavior, and timing under OS-level execution. In-process mocked timing commonly hides drops/lag failure modes. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML decision chain is involved; this is infrastructure/performance behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a system load test against the built tool process using a real high-rate input stream (10k entries/s) for a sustained interval; measure ingest rate, end-to-end lag, and total processed vs sent counts, failing if any entries are dropped or backlog grows beyond allowed bounds.
